summary: Chunk Eckhart Sermons (CCEL) — regex-section-split on PREFACE/Roman-numeral sermon titles
description:
Source: raw/christian_mysticism/eckhart-sermons-field.txt — 67,556 chars, single file (format=html, Field 1909, CCEL).

Audit findings:
- Head: CCEL page-number marker '1 2-3 HEART AND LIFE BOOKLETS...' then title page, then '4-5 PREFACE'.
- Tail: 'Index of Scripture References' + 'Index of Pages of the Print Edition 1 2-3 4-5 ... 62-63 64-65' nav block.
- Section markers: clean Roman-numeral sermon titles — 'I THE ATTRACTIVE POWER OF GOD', 'II THE NEARNESS OF THE KINGDOM', ..., 'VII OUTWARD AND INWARD MORALITY' (7 sermons + 1 PREFACE = 8 sections).
- Per-page pollution: CCEL print-edition page markers '2-3', '4-5', '10-11', ..., '64-65' scattered throughout text. Pattern '\b\d+-\d+\b' catches them safely (scripture refs use colons like 'Luke 1:28', no collateral damage).

Recommended strategy: regex-section-split.

pattern: '^(PREFACE|[IVX]+\s+[A-Z][A-Z ]+)'

pre_strip:
  - '\b\d+-\d+\b' inline page markers
  - leading 'HEART AND LIFE BOOKLETS...' front matter (or keep as separate front-matter chunk — your call)
  - trailing 'Indexes Index of Scripture References...Index of Pages...' block

NOTE: this is the first CCEL source. If the CCEL print-edition page-marker pattern recurs in future CCEL sources, promote it to a shared 'ccel' extractor profile in chunk_init.py.

resolution.note: Wrote chunking/christian_mysticism/eckhart-sermons-field.toml with regex-section-split on PREFACE + Roman numeral (I..VII) headers. Regex required iteration: Sermon IV is 'TRUE HEARING' (no THE prefix), so the lookahead accepts either 'THE\s+' or 4+ all-caps word; PREFACE bypass the lookahead since it precedes a quoted lowercase opener. pre_strip handles CCEL print-edition page markers (\b\d+-\d+\b), the leading HEART AND LIFE BOOKLETS title page, the trailing 'Indexes Index of Scripture References' block, and the ECKHART'S SERMONS connector phrase. 23 chunks (14866 tokens, avg 646/chunk) across 8 sections with subsplits.
